implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-002: every §4 contract has a contract test
AC-003: every seam has a seam test
AC-004: subject regex assertions match §4 patterns exactly
AC-007: correlate_outcome two calls = equal records + one UPDATE

completion_promises.evidence: Created tests/forge/dispatch/test_contract_and_seam.py with 29 tests organised into 5 contract-test classes and 5 seam-test classes. Mirrors the structure of tests/forge/test_contract_and_seam.py (TASK-NFI-010).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each §4 Integration Contract has at least one contract test in this module.
completion_promises.evidence: Five contract-test classes cover the five §4 Integration Contracts in the task spec: TestCapabilityResolutionContract (CapabilityResolution incl. retry_of round-trip), TestCorrelationKeyContract (CorrelationKey format invariant), TestDispatchOutcomeSumTypeContract (every variant survives model_dump/model_validate via TypeAdapter discriminator), TestDispatchEnvelopeContract (subject + reply regex), TestCorrelateOutcomeIdempotencyContract (correlate_outcome idempotency).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each seam in the table has at least one seam test in this module.
completion_promises.evidence: Five seam-test classes cover the five rows in the seam table: TestDispatchDiscoverySeam (dispatch reads only cache.snapshot, never internals), TestDispatchPersistenceSeam (sensitive parameter values never appear in any persisted column), TestDispatchNatsAdapterImportSeam (orchestrator does not import nats; adapter is sole site), TestCorrelationAdapterOrderingSeam (subscribe-before-publish), TestOutcomeDownstreamSeam (correlate_outcome signature + idempotency stable for FEAT-FORGE-004).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Subject-format tests use regex assertions with the exact patterns declared in IMPLEMENTATION-GUIDE.md §4.
completion_promises.evidence: Module-level constants COMMAND_SUBJECT_RE = re.compile(r'^agents\.command\.[a-z0-9-]+$') and RESULT_SUBJECT_RE = re.compile(r'^agents\.result\.[a-z0-9-]+\.[0-9a-f]{32}$') are pinned as the single source of truth and used by TestDispatchEnvelopeContract — including positive matches against COMMAND_SUBJECT_TEMPLATE / RESULT_SUBJECT_TEMPLATE and the static command_subject_for/result_subject_for helpers, plus negative tests that reject uppercase agent_id and 31/33-char correlation keys.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Import-boundary seam test: a Python AST-level test verifies that forge/dispatch/*.py does NOT import nats (any submodule). Use ast.parse() + walk imports — do NOT do a string grep.
completion_promises.evidence: TestDispatchNatsAdapterImportSeam.test_no_dispatch_module_imports_nats walks every .py file under src/forge/dispatch via ast.parse(), iterates ast.walk() for ast.Import and ast.ImportFrom nodes, and asserts no module name equals 'nats' or starts with 'nats.'. A companion test (test_no_dispatch_module_uses_importlib_for_nats) also AST-walks ast.Call nodes for dynamic imports of 'nats*' string constants. nats_core (the schema package) is intentionally allowed and not flagged.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (CorrelationKey distinctness): 1000 generated keys are unique and all match [0-9a-f]{32}.
completion_promises.evidence: TestCorrelationKeyContract.test_one_thousand_keys_are_unique_and_match_format constructs a CorrelationRegistry over an inert ReplyChannel, generates 1000 keys via fresh_correlation_key(), asserts len(set(keys)) == 1000, and validates every key against re.compile(r'^[0-9a-f]{32}$').fullmatch.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (correlate_outcome idempotency): two consecutive calls with the same args produce the same record AND issue exactly one database UPDATE.
completion_promises.evidence: TestCorrelateOutcomeIdempotencyContract.test_two_calls_return_equal_records_and_one_update seeds a resolution row, wraps the SqliteHistoryWriter._connection with _UpdateCountingConnection (which counts SQL strings starting with UPDATE), invokes correlate_outcome twice with identical args, asserts the returned records are equal and that counter.update_count == 1.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: ruff check tests/forge/dispatch/test_contract_and_seam.py: 'All checks passed!' (after removing one unused import). ruff format would reformat several files including the new one, but the same is true for every existing test file in tests/forge/dispatch/ — the project does not enforce ruff format. Lint is clean.
